title: Annealing
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Annealing process used in stainless steel manufacturing to restore ductility, relieve internal stresses, and improve the mechanical properties of cold rolled material.
learningObjectives: Learn how Annealing improves stainless steel properties.
Identify the major equipment used during Annealing.

content.introduction: Cold Rolling increases the strength of stainless steel but also makes it harder and less ductile. Annealing is a heat treatment process that restores ductility, relieves internal stresses, and prepares the material for further processing.
content.purpose: The primary objective of Annealing is to soften the material, improve its microstructure, restore formability, and prepare it for Pickling and finishing operations.
content.workingPrinciple: The stainless steel strip passes through a controlled furnace where it is heated to a specific temperature, held for a predetermined time, and then cooled under controlled conditions to obtain the desired material properties.
processSteps: Load material into the Annealing Line.
content.summary: Annealing restores the required mechanical properties of stainless steel after Cold Rolling. Proper temperature control and cooling ensure consistent quality and prepare the material for Pickling.
